items.title: Profilers ladder
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  cP["cProfile (deterministic, function-level)"] --> sn["snakeviz (visualizer)"]
  cP --> lp["line_profiler (line-level)"]
  cP --> pys["py-spy (sampling, prod-safe)"]
  cP --> mp["memray / tracemalloc (memory)"]
  style cP fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a
items.caption: Start with cProfile to triage; reach for the right specialised tool based on what cProfile reveals.

items.title: tottime vs cumtime — how to read the columns
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  A["function A — cumtime 5s, tottime 0.1s"] -->|its children are slow| B["function B — cumtime 4.9s, tottime 4.8s"]
  C["sort by cumulative"] -. surfaces A .-> A
  D["sort by tottime"] -. surfaces B .-> B
  Note["Optimise where tottime is high — that's where the real work is."]
items.caption: A high-cumulative low-tottime function is usually just a router; the optimisation usually lives one or two levels deeper, where tottime concentrates.
